title: Richly Roasted French Pork Cassoulet
description: A hearty, slow-cooked French dish featuring caramelized sugar sauce and a cheesy crust, perfect for a lazy Sunday dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the pork shoulder with salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat and sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides.
Remove the pork from the pot and set it aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is caramelized and golden brown.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the tomato paste to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth to the pot and bring it to a boil, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Return the pork shoulder to the pot and add the white beans, thyme, and salt and pepper to taste.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Roast the cassoulet in the oven for 2 1/2 hours, or until the pork is tender and the top is crispy and golden brown.
